zh.of Glyn-Nedd, Glamorgan, Wales: 英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根
英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根
zh.lifeSummary: 當Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd於大約1275年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根時，他的父親Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen47歲，母親Vaughan37歲。他和Joan ap Evan至少有7個兒子和1個女兒。

zh-hans.of Glyn-Nedd, Glamorgan, Wales: 英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根
英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd于大约1275年出生在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根时，他的父亲Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen47岁，母亲Vaughan37岁。他和Joan ap Evan至少有7个儿子和1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: from a short form of the personal name Adelhard, which is of ancient Germanic origin, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + hard ‘bold, hardy’. Compare Ale and Alma 4.
Swedish: variant of   Ahl  .

variants.name: Ale
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos Enrique, Cesar, Jose, Maria De Jesus.
Spanish (Andalusia; Alé): unexplained.
Italian (Sicily; Alé): probably a variant of Aleo .

nameMeanings.name: Ioworth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of   Howarth  .

variants.name: Howarth
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Howarth in Rochdale (Lancashire), possibly from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + worth ‘enclosure’. The name may have been confused with Haworth .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Howel
meanings: The English form of the Welsh name Hywel.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Rhys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘ardour’; borne in the Middle Ages by various rulers in south-west Wales, including  Rhys ap Tewdur  (  d. 1093 ) and  Rhys ap Gruffudd  (  1132–97 ).

variants.name: Reece
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Rhys . The name is now in widespread and frequent use outside Wales, in many cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the given name.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: Anglicized spelling of Welsh Rhys, in some cases representing a transferred use of the surname so spelled, which is derived from the Welsh given name.

nameMeanings.name: Vychan
meanings: Welsh: A Welsh name of great antiquity, from vyckan, little, small; i.e. small in stature. Compare the surname Bychan and the Armoric Le Bihan.

en.lifeSummary: When Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd was born about 1275, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen, was 47 and his mother, Vaughan, was 37. He had at least 7 sons and 1 daughter with Joan ap Evan.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd est né environ 1275, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Vaughan, avait 37 ans. Il a eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille avec Joan ap Evan.

de.lifeSummary: Als Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd um 1275 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen 47 und seine Mutter Vaughan 37. Er hatte mit Joan ap Evan mindestens 7 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

ht.of Glyn-Nedd, Glamorgan, Wales: Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom
Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, United Kingdom

it.lifeSummary: Quando Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd è nato il circa 1275 nel Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Vaughan, aveva 37 anni. Lui avuto almeno 7 figli e 1 figlia con Joan ap Evan.

イギリスウェールズグラモーガン
ja.lifeSummary: Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlydが約1275年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた時，父Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwenは47歳，母Vaughanは37歳であった。彼はJoan ap Evanとの間に少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd(이)가 대략 1275년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Vaughan(은)는 37세였다. 그는 Joan ap Evan(와)과 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd nasceu aproximadamente 1275, em Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Vaughan, tinha 37 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha com Joan ap Evan.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd родился около 1275 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Vaughan, 37. У него было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь от Joan ap Evan.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Howel ap Rhys Vychan ap Ioworth of Cerf-Y-Ddwy-Gwlyd nació aproximadamente 1275, en Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Rhys Foel ap Rhys Of Gilfachwen, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Vaughan, tenía 37 años. Tuvo por lo menos 7 hijos y 1 hija con Joan ap Evan.
